title: Database Management System
description: Practice year-wise MCQs from 2018 to 2026 with explanations and answer review.

questions.prompt: Which key uniquely identifies a row in a table?
questions.explanation: A primary key uniquely identifies each row in a database table.
First normal form eliminates repeating groups and keeps each column atomic.

questions.prompt: Which operation combines rows from two tables based on a common condition?
questions.explanation: A join combines rows from multiple tables using a related condition.

questions.prompt: A foreign key is used to:
questions.explanation: A foreign key links a column in one table to a primary key in another table.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a DDL command?
questions.explanation: CREATE is a data definition language command used to define database objects.
Normalization organizes data to avoid unnecessary duplication and anomalies.

questions.prompt: Which SQL clause is used to filter rows after grouping?
questions.explanation: HAVING filters grouped rows after aggregate operations.

options: It is completed fully or not at all
questions.answer: It is completed fully or not at all
questions.explanation: Atomicity ensures all operations of a transaction are treated as a single unit.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a candidate key?
questions.explanation: A candidate key is a minimal superkey that can uniquely identify a row.
